channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran Calls for Reparations and International Guarantees Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: In a significant diplomatic move, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has called for reparations and firm international guarantees against future aggression, labelling these as the only way to end the...

item.title: Authorities urged to enforce urban planning and protect riverbanks after Nairobi floods
item.description: The recent floods in Nairobi, which claimed 49 lives and caused widespread property damage, have highlighted the dangers posed by unregulated urban development. Rogue developers constructing buildings...

item.title: American Visitor Shares Experience Discussing Politics in a Northern Ireland Pub
item.description: A TikTok creator known as @lawcroxnotn shared a video describing her experience as a Trump supporter visiting a pub in Northern Ireland. According to her, the atmosphere shifted when a local patron re...

item.title: IGP Disu Reassigns DIG Onwuemelie to Logistics Following Human Rights Complaints
item.description: The Inspector General of Police, IGP Olatunji Disu, don redeploy DIG Kenechukwu Onwuemelie from Force Intelligence Department (FID) to the Logistics Department because e human rights record no too cle...

item.title: Keir Starmer vows government action against unfair heating oil price rises in Northern Ireland due to Middle East conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has stated that the UK government will not allow suppliers to exploit the ongoing Middle East crisis for profit, particularly in the home heating oil market. During a v...

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Threaten Vegetable Supply in Cordillera, Prices May Increase
item.description: Vegetable prices in the Philippines, particularly in the Cordillera region, may increase due to rising fuel costs that are making farming and transportation less profitable for farmers. Farmers like J...

item.title: UK Man Admits Encouraging Vulnerable American to Commit Suicide Over Video Call
item.description: A 21-year-old man from Morley, West Yorkshire, Dylan Phelan, has admitted to encouraging a vulnerable American man, Travis Dyer of Louisiana, to take his own life during a video call on October 30, 20...

item.title: NERC Updates Guidelines for Discos on Handling Meter Tampering and Illegal Electricity Access
item.description: The Nigerian Electricity Regulatory Commission (NERC) don update the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) wey Discos go follow to tackle unauthorised electricity use, meter tampering, and power bypass a...

item.title: Wike Urges Transparency in PDP Peace Talks, Rejects Night-Time Negotiations
item.description: The FCT Minister, Nyesom Wike, don yarn serious matter about reconciliation inside the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). During the 106th National Executive Committee (NEC) meeting wey the Abdulrahman M...

item.title: Tanzania Plans National Water Grid to Connect Major Lakes and Rivers
item.description: Tanzania is moving fast with plans to establish a National Water Grid, which is meant to link major water sources across the country to make sure there is enough water for people and economic growth. ...

item.title: Orlando Pirates move to the top of the PSL log with 2-0 win over Richards Bay
item.description: Orlando Pirates have taken a big step towards the Betway Premiership title by beating Richards Bay 2-0 at the Orlando Amstel Arena in Soweto. This win puts them on 47 points, level with Mamelodi Sundo...

item.title: Evaluating the Potential Value of Kyler Murray and Tua Tagovailoa in NFL Free Agency
item.description: Kyler Murray and Tua Tagovailoa are emerging as notable value picks in NFL free agency, thanks to their relatively low cost despite high potential. The Atlanta Falcons acquired Tagovailoa for a modest...

item.title: Election Commission to Directly Manage Security Deployment in West Bengal Polls
item.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has decided that police observers appointed by the Commission will now have full authority to manage the deployment of both Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) an...

item.title: IEA to release record 400 million barrels of oil to stabilise market amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has announced a historic release of 400 million barrels from its emergency oil reserves, marking the largest such action in its history. This collective decision ...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal Residents Can Apply for Smart IDs at Select Bank Branches
item.description: Residents of KwaZulu-Natal can now apply for smart ID cards at certain bank branches, following a new partnership between the South African government and major banking institutions. This development ...

item.title: Jersey investigates whether Abramovich's Chelsea sale proceeds may be proceeds of crime amid ongoing UK dispute
item.description: The frozen proceeds from Roman Abramovich's 2022 sale of Chelsea FC, totaling £2.407 billion, are under investigation in Jersey to determine if they constitute proceeds of crime, according to recent a...

item.title: Initial Release of Mandelson Files Reveals Concerns Over Appointment and Epstein Links
item.description: The initial release of the Mandelson files has exposed significant concerns over the vetting and appointment of Lord Mandelson as a UK government envoy, though it did not reveal a major scandal in its...

item.title: ACT driver disputes seatbelt fine after hair obstructed camera view
item.description: Lanie Tindale, a Canberra driver, was fined $423 and penalised 12 points by NSW authorities for allegedly not wearing her seatbelt correctly. The incident occurred on the Hume Highway late at night ju...

item.title: Teenager convicted for robbing tourist of luxury watch in Mayfair
item.description: In February 2024, Mohamed Sellaoui, a 17-year-old Algerian illegal immigrant, stalked and attacked a Chinese tourist in Mayfair, London, to steal a luxury Patek Philippe watch valued at £37,000. The v...

item.title: Urgent Measures Needed to Curb Kenya's Ballooning Public Debt
item.description: Kenya's public debt has become a big worry as it keeps shooting up, now standing at nearly 70% of GDP, way above what experts say is safe. The editorial calls on the government to take serious action ...
